display_prompt:
### SYSTEM
You are an expert data engineer specializing in converting data to text. You will be given the current Python program with its BLUE score and an inspiration program and your task will be to evolve the current Python program to improve the score. It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: Airport. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:
Triples:
(Antwerp | cityServed | Antwerp International Airport)
(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)
Example sentence:
"Antwerp International Airport serves the city of Antwerp which is in Belgium, where the capital is Brussels."

The 'predict' function returns that sentence as a string. Below is the list of all possible predicates that can be given as an input. With the given predicates and example triples implement the 'predict' function so it can process all the possible predicates:

Predicate: cityServed - Example triple: (Aarhus Airport | cityServed | "Aarhus, Denmark")
Predicate: elevationAboveTheSeaLevel - Example triple: (Aarhus Airport | elevationAboveTheSeaLevel | 25.0)
Predicate: location - Example triple: (Aarhus Airport | location | Tirstrup)

Predicate: isPartOf - Example triple: (Abilene, Texas | isPartOf | Jones County, Texas)
Predicate: 1stRunwayLengthFeet - Example triple: (Abilene Regional Airport | 1stRunwayLengthFeet | 3678)

The THEMIS review is:

It also correctly identifies that Greenville is part of both Ellington, Wisconsin, and Menasha (town), Wisconsin, and that it is in the United States. The text does a good job of combining the information from the triples into a coherent sentence structure, with the majority of the information conveyed in a single complex sentence. There are no additional pieces of information that were not present in the triples. However, the readability of the sentence could be improved by breaking it down into two sentences or using a comma to separate the clauses more clearly, as it can be slightly confusing due to its length and complexity.

Based on the review try to understand why the program might have performed poorly and improve the program accordingly.

# Current Program
```python
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""

    import re

    def clean(text: str) -> str:
        t = str(text).strip()
        if len(t) >= 2 and t[0] == '"' and t[-1] == '"':
            t = t[1:-1]
        return t

    def join_parts(parts: list[str]) -> str:
        if not parts:
            return ""
        if len(parts) == 1:
            return parts[0]
        if len(parts) == 2:
            return f"{parts[0]} and {parts[1]}"
        return f"{', '.join(parts[:-1])}, and {parts[-1]}"

    def humanize_predicate(p: str) -> str:
        x = p.replace("_", " ")
        x = re.sub(r"([a-z])([A-Z])", r"\1 \2", x)
        return x.lower()

    templates = {
        "cityServed": "serves {o}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "has an elevation of {o} metres above sea level",
        "location": "is located in {o}",
        "operatingOrganisation": "is operated by {o}",
        "runwayLength": "has a runway length of {o} metres",
        "runwayName": "has runway {o}",
        "country": "is in {o}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {o}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "has the ICAO location identifier {o}",
        "locationIdentifier": "has the location identifier {o}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "has an elevation of {o} feet above sea level",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "has the IATA location identifier {o}",
        "nativeName": "is also known as {o}",
        "leaderParty": "has {o} as the leader party",
        "capital": "has capital {o}",
        "language": "uses {o}",
        "leader": "is led by {o}",
        "owner": "is owned by {o}",
        "largestCity": "has largest city {o}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "has an elevation of {o} metres above sea level",
        "administrativeArrondissement": "is in the arrondissement of {o}",
        "mayor": "has mayor {o}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "has a runway surface of {o}",
        "officialLanguage": "has official language {o}",
        "city": "is in {o}",
        "jurisdiction": "has jurisdiction over {o}",
        "demonym": "has the demonym {o}",
        "aircraftHelicopter": "uses {o} as a helicopter",
        "transportAircraft": "uses {o} as a transport aircraft",
        "currency": "uses the currency {o}",
        "headquarter": "is headquartered at {o}",
        "class": "belongs to class {o}",
        "division": "belongs to division {o}",
        "order": "belongs to order {o}",
        "regionServed": "serves {o}",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title {o}",
        "hubAirport": "has hub airport {o}",
        "aircraftFighter": "uses {o} as a fighter aircraft",
        "attackAircraft": "uses {o} as an attack aircraft",
        "battle": "fought in {o}",
        "countySeat": "has county seat {o}",
        "chief": "has chief {o}",
        "foundedBy": "was founded by {o}",
        "postalCode": "has postal code {o}",
        "areaCode": "has area code {o}",
        "foundingYear": "was founded in {o}",
        "ceremonialCounty": "is in the ceremonial county of {o}",
    }

    grouped: dict[str, dict[str, list[str]]] = {}
    subject_order: list[str] = []

    for t in triples:
        s = clean(t.subject)
        p = t.predicate
        o = clean(t.object)
        if s not in grouped:
            grouped[s] = {}
            subject_order.append(s)
        grouped[s].setdefault(p, [])
        if o not in grouped[s][p]:
            grouped[s][p].append(o)

    def build_phrase(p: str, objs: list[str]) -> str:
        m = re.match(r"^(\d+(?:st|nd|rd|th))Runway(LengthFeet|LengthMetre|SurfaceType|Number)$", p)
        if m:
            idx, kind = m.groups()
            if kind == "LengthFeet":
                vals = join_parts([f"{x} feet" for x in objs])
                return f"has its {idx} runway length at {vals}"
            if kind == "LengthMetre":
                vals = join_parts([f"{x} metres" for x in objs])
                return f"has its {idx} runway length at {vals}"
            if kind == "SurfaceType":
                return f"has {join_parts(objs)} as the surface type of its {idx} runway"
            return f"has {join_parts(objs)} as the number of its {idx} runway"

        o_joined = join_parts(objs)
        if p in templates:
            return templates[p].format(o=o_joined)
        return f"has {humanize_predicate(p)} {o_joined}"

    def subject_sentence(s: str) -> str:
        phrases = [build_phrase(p, objs) for p, objs in grouped[s].items()]
        return f"{s} {join_parts(phrases)}"

    known_subjects = set(grouped.keys())
    if len(subject_order) == 1:
        return subject_sentence(subject_order[0]) + "."

    start = subject_order[0]
    used = {start}
    where_clauses: list[str] = []

    changed = True
    while changed:
        changed = False
        for us in list(used):
            for objs in grouped[us].values():
                for o in objs:
                    if o in known_subjects and o not in used:
                        where_clauses.append(f"where {subject_sentence(o)}")
                        used.add(o)
                        changed = True

    remaining = [s for s in subject_order if s not in used]
    text = subject_sentence(start)
    if where_clauses:
        text += ", " + ", ".join(where_clauses)
    if remaining:
        text += ", and " + ", and ".join(subject_sentence(s) for s in remaining)
    return text + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
```

        "demonym": "has the demonym {o}",

        "transportAircraft": "uses {o} as a transport aircraft",
        "currency": "uses the currency {o}",
        "headquarter": "is headquartered at {o}",
        "class": "belongs to class {o}",
        "division": "belongs to division {o}",
        "order": "belongs to order {o}",
        "regionServed": "serves {o}",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title {o}",
        "hubAirport": "has hub airport {o}",
        "aircraftFighter": "uses {o} as a fighter aircraft",
        "attackAircraft": "uses {o} as an attack aircraft",
        "battle": "fought in {o}",
        "countySeat": "has county seat {o}",
        "chief": "has chief {o}",
        "foundedBy": "was founded by {o}",
        "postalCode": "has postal code {o}",
        "areaCode": "has area code {o}",
        "foundingYear": "was founded in {o}",
        "ceremonialCounty": "is in the ceremonial county of {o}",
    }

    grouped: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        s = clean(t.subject)
        p = t.predicate
        o = clean(t.object)

        m = re.match(r"^(\d+(?:st|nd|rd|th))Runway(LengthFeet|LengthMetre|SurfaceType|Number)$", p)
        if m:
            idx, kind = m.groups()
            if kind == "LengthFeet":
                phrase = f"has its {idx} runway length at {o} feet"
            elif kind == "LengthMetre":
                phrase = f"has its {idx} runway length at {o} metres"
            elif kind == "SurfaceType":
                phrase = f"has {o} as the surface type of its {idx} runway"
            else:
                phrase = f"has {o} as the number of its {idx} runway"
        elif p in templates:
            phrase = templates[p].format(o=o)
        else:
            phrase = f"has {humanize_predicate(p)} {o}"

        grouped.setdefault(s, []).append(phrase)

    subject_sentences: list[str] = []
    for s, phrases in grouped.items():
        unique_phrases = list(dict.fromkeys(phrases))
        subject_sentences.append(f"{s} {join_parts(unique_phrases)}")

    if len(subject_sentences) == 1:
        return subject_sentences[0] + "."

    return "; ".join(subject_sentences[:-1]) + "; and " + subject_sentences[-1] + "."

```python
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""

    import re

    def clean(text: str) -> str:
        t = str(text).strip()
        if len(t) >= 2 and t[0] == '"' and t[-1] == '"':
            t = t[1:-1]
        return t

    def join_parts(parts: list[str]) -> str:
        if not parts:
            return ""
        if len(parts) == 1:
            return parts[0]
        if len(parts) == 2:
            return f"{parts[0]} and {parts[1]}"
        return f"{', '.join(parts[:-1])}, and {parts[-1]}"

    def humanize_predicate(p: str) -> str:
        x = p.replace("_", " ")
        x = re.sub(r"([a-z])([A-Z])", r"\1 \2", x)
        return x.lower()

    templates = {
        "cityServed": "serves {o}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "has an elevation of {o} metres above sea level",
        "location": "is located in {o}",
        "operatingOrganisation": "is operated by {o}",
        "runwayLength": "has a runway length of {o} metres",
        "runwayName": "has runway {o}",
        "country": "is in {o}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {o}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "has the ICAO location identifier {o}",
        "locationIdentifier": "has the location identifier {o}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "has an elevation of {o} feet above sea level",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "has the IATA location identifier {o}",
        "nativeName": "is also known as {o}",
        "leaderParty": "has {o} as the leader party",
        "capital": "has capital {o}",
        "language": "uses {o}",
        "leader": "is led by {o}",
        "owner": "is owned by {o}",
        "largestCity": "has largest city {o}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "has an elevation of {o} metres above sea level",
        "administrativeArrondissement": "is in the arrondissement of {o}",
        "mayor": "has mayor {o}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "has a runway surface of {o}",
        "officialLanguage": "has official language {o}",
        "city": "is in {o}",
        "jurisdiction": "has jurisdiction over {o}",
        "demonym": "has the demonym {o}",
        "aircraftHelicopter": "uses {o} as a helicopter",
        "transportAircraft": "uses {o} as a transport aircraft",
        "currency": "uses the currency {o}",
        "headquarter": "is headquartered at {o}",
        "class": "belongs to class {o}",
        "division": "belongs to division {o}",
        "order": "belongs to order {o}",
        "regionServed": "serves {o}",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title {o}",
        "hubAirport": "has hub airport {o}",
        "aircraftFighter": "uses {o} as a fighter aircraft",
        "attackAircraft": "uses {o} as an attack aircraft",
        "battle": "fought in {o}",
        "countySeat": "has county seat {o}",
        "chief": "has chief {o}",
        "foundedBy": "was founded by {o}",
        "postalCode": "has postal code {o}",
        "areaCode": "has area code {o}",
        "foundingYear": "was founded in {o}",
        "ceremonialCounty": "is in the ceremonial county of {o}",
    }

    grouped: dict[str, dict[str, list[str]]] = {}
    subject_order: list[str] = []

    for t in triples:
        s = clean(t.subject)
        p = t.predicate
        o = clean(t.object)
        if s not in grouped:
            grouped[s] = {}
            subject_order.append(s)
        grouped[s].setdefault(p, [])
        if o not in grouped[s][p]:
            grouped[s][p].append(o)

    def build_phrase(p: str, objs: list[str]) -> str:
        m = re.match(r"^(\d+(?:st|nd|rd|th))Runway(LengthFeet|LengthMetre|SurfaceType|Number)$", p)
        if m:
            idx, kind = m.groups()
            if kind == "LengthFeet":
                vals = join_parts([f"{x} feet" for x in objs])
                return f"has its {idx} runway length at {vals}"
            if kind == "LengthMetre":
                vals = join_parts([f"{x} metres" for x in objs])
                return f"has its {idx} runway length at {vals}"
            if kind == "SurfaceType":
                return f"has {join_parts(objs)} as the surface type of its {idx} runway"
            return f"has {join_parts(objs)} as the number of its {idx} runway"

        o_joined = join_parts(objs)
        if p in templates:
            return templates[p].format(o=o_joined)
        return f"has {humanize_predicate(p)} {o_joined}"

    def subject_sentence(s: str) -> str:
        phrases = [build_phrase(p, objs) for p, objs in grouped[s].items()]
        return f"{s} {join_parts(phrases)}"

    known_subjects = set(grouped.keys())
    if len(subject_order) == 1:
        return subject_sentence(subject_order[0]) + "."

    start = subject_order[0]
    used = {start}
    where_clauses: list[str] = []

    changed = True
    while changed:
        changed = False
        for us in list(used):
            for objs in grouped[us].values():
                for o in objs:
                    if o in known_subjects and o not in used:
                        where_clauses.append(f"where {subject_sentence(o)}")
                        used.add(o)
                        changed = True

    remaining = [s for s in subject_order if s not in used]
    text = subject_sentence(start)
    if where_clauses:
        text += ", " + ", ".join(where_clauses)
    if remaining:
        text += ", and " + ", and ".join(subject_sentence(s) for s in remaining)
    return text + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
```
